methods.description: Sell your product directly to customers through your own website or a marketplace. Highest margin, full brand control, no middleman.
methods.first_step: Set up a GoDaddy Online Store or list on Goldbelly (built for artisan food brands). Add your store link to every social profile and post. This is your primary revenue channel.

methods.description: Get your product on restaurant tables and store shelves. Wholesale accounts create recurring, predictable revenue without relying on social media.
methods.first_step: Identify 10 local restaurants or specialty food shops in your area. Use the Brand Collab DM template to pitch your product for wholesale. Offer a free sample case — most buyers need to taste before they commit.

methods.description: In-person sales at markets and pop-ups build brand recognition, generate content, and convert buyers into loyal online customers.
methods.first_step: Apply to 2–3 local farmers markets or food fairs this month. Each market appearance generates content, new followers, and direct sales. Bring a sign-up sheet for your email list.

methods.description: Turn one-time buyers into monthly revenue with a hot sauce club or subscription box. Predictable cash flow and higher customer lifetime value.
methods.first_step: Set up a monthly subscription on your store (GoDaddy or Shopify supports this natively). Start with a 'Hot Sauce of the Month' offering — one new or seasonal flavor each month at a discounted bundle price.

methods.description: Companies buy branded food products in bulk for client gifts, employee perks, and event giveaways. High-volume orders with minimal marketing effort.
methods.first_step: Create a 'Corporate Gifting' page on your website with bulk pricing tiers (12-pack, 24-pack, custom labels). Reach out to local businesses directly via LinkedIn or email — companies order in Q4 especially.

methods.description: Partner with complementary food brands, restaurants, or local businesses for co-branded products, joint promotions, or featured placement.
methods.first_step: Identify 5 brands whose audience matches yours (a local taco shop, a craft beer brand, a BBQ rub company). Propose a co-marketing post, a bundle deal, or a joint giveaway. No payment needed — just audience sharing.

where_to_start_by_followers.0-999: Focus on direct sales first — set up your online store and apply to one local farmers market this month. Every in-person sale builds brand recognition that no ad spend can replicate.
where_to_start_by_followers.1000-9999: You have enough of an audience to pitch wholesale accounts. Send samples to 5 local restaurants this week — wholesale creates the recurring revenue that stabilizes a product business.
where_to_start_by_followers.10000-99999: Launch a subscription option. You have the customer base to sustain a monthly recurring product, and subscription revenue is the most predictable income a product brand can have.
where_to_start_by_followers.100000+: Corporate gifting and national retail are your next channels. At this scale, one retail placement or a single corporate gifting contract can outperform months of direct online sales.
